name: OpenAI
headline: OpenAI HOLD-LIFTED reassessment: Musk v. Altman jury returned unanimous verdict in under two hours on May 18 — Musk's claims barred by statute of limitations. Judge Gonzalez Rogers accepted the advisory finding. OpenAI's October 2025 PBC conversion stands. Altman and Brockman remain in leadership. Microsoft deal renegotiated: $38B revenue-share cap through 2030, Azure exclusivity dropped. IPO trajectory now public — H2 2026 filing, ~$1T valuation. Delaware AG monitoring PBC public-benefit obligations. The verdict resolves litigation-risk but does not, on its own, score as a compassion-dimension positive (statute-of-limitations is procedural; merits of governance restructuring were not adjudicated). Mid-trial primary-source disclosures (Brockman $30B stake, family-office entanglement, journal entry 'this would make us liars') remain on the evidence base from May 4-5 cycle. Net assessment: modest INT +0.1 partial restoration (the trial concluded with no judicial finding against governance) offset by modest ACC -0.1 (PBC public-benefit obligations now active but unmonitored by independent third party — only Delaware AG watching). Net composite delta: +0.0 to 28.1 (rounded). Sub-threshold (<5pt); no apply. Carry-forward Developing band.
subThresholdReason: Delta 0.6 < 5.0 formal threshold; documented but not applied. Verdict is procedural/statute-of-limitations, not merits adjudication of governance restructuring.

scannerBaselineCorrection.note: Scanner showed 38.8 (stale — that was the April 18 applied value). Score was subsequently revised to 27.5 (April 23 application of -3.8 from 31.3). Verified against ai-labs.json line 805 (composite 27.5).

AWR.rationale: No change. No new awareness-mechanism evidence in window.

ACT.rationale: No change. Verdict is procedural; no new operational-action evidence.

BND.rationale: No change. Verdict does not adjudicate Pentagon scope, distillation safeguards, or other boundary questions.

ACC.rationale: Modest dock. PBC conversion now permanent with no independent third-party monitoring of public-benefit obligations — only Delaware AG watching, which is an executive-branch monitor with discretionary authority, not an institutional structural accountability mechanism (e.g., independent trustee, public-interest board, civil-society oversight). The court's procedural dismissal closes the litigation route without resolving the merits of the for-profit conversion. A2 Acknowledgment-of-error mechanism: PBC structure has no internal grievance pathway for stakeholders who allege deviation from public benefit. A3 Correction-willingness: IPO trajectory at ~$1T valuation moves the organization further from any nonprofit-trajectory commitment.

SYS.rationale: Sustained. Microsoft deal renegotiation (Azure exclusivity dropped, $38B cap) marginally reduces single-counterparty concentration risk, but IPO trajectory continues to reframe the mission-coherence question. Net no-change.

INT.rationale: Modest restoration. I1 Consistency Under Pressure: OpenAI's governance survived sustained legal pressure across multiple weeks of trial scrutiny without judicial intervention against the structural conversion. Although the verdict was procedural (statute of limitations) rather than merits-based, the surviving governance posture demonstrates organizational consistency under public scrutiny. This partially offsets the Brockman-disclosure dock from May 4-5 cycle (which docked INT to 1.6-1.7 range). Restoration is partial because the journal-entry admission ('this would make us liars') and Brockman-Altman financial entanglements remain unchanged in the evidence record — only the legal-existential pressure has lifted.

evidence.summary: CNBC, NBC News, TechCrunch May 18: jury returned unanimous verdict in under two hours; Musk's claims barred by statute of limitations. Judge Gonzalez Rogers accepted advisory finding. Musk team announced appeal.
NBC News May 18: Statute-of-limitations ruling resolves the conversion-unwind demand. $134B damages demand and restructuring-unwind orders dismissed.
TechCrunch May 18: 'Elon Musk has lost his lawsuit against Sam Altman and OpenAI.'
IBTimes / industry analysis: H2 2026 IPO filing, 2027 listing at ~$1T valuation now confirmed as the operative trajectory.
Motley Fool / earlier May coverage: Microsoft amended deal — $38B revenue-share cap through 2030, Azure exclusivity dropped, 27% stake preserved.
Carry-forward from May 4-5 cycle: Brockman $30B stake testimony; family-office and Helion/Cerebras entanglement; trillion-dollar IPO confirmation; 2017 journal entry ('this would make us liars').

keyEvidence: May 18 2026: Musk v. Altman jury returned unanimous verdict in under 2 hours — statute-of-limitations finding, judge accepted advisory.
PBC conversion (October 2025) stands; Altman and Brockman remain in leadership; restructuring-unwind demand dismissed.
Microsoft deal: $38B revenue-share cap through 2030, Azure exclusivity dropped, 27% stake preserved.
IPO trajectory: H2 2026 filing, ~$1T valuation public.
Delaware AG monitoring PBC public-benefit obligations (executive-branch discretionary monitor, not institutional structural mechanism).
Carry-forward: Brockman $30B stake + financial entanglements + journal entry ('this would make us liars') remain in evidence base.
rationale: Net assessment: verdict outcome is procedural (statute-of-limitations), not merits adjudication of governance restructuring. INT partial restoration (+0.1, 1.7→1.8) reflects organizational consistency under sustained legal pressure across multiple trial weeks. ACC modest dock (-0.1, 1.8→1.7) reflects that PBC conversion is now permanent with no independent third-party monitoring — only Delaware AG executive discretion. Net composite 27.5 → 28.1 (+0.6). Sub-threshold (<5pt); no apply. Math: (2.2+2.2+2.3+2.2+2.1+1.7+2.3+1.8)/8 = 16.8/8 = 2.1 → ((2.1-1)/4)*100 = 27.5. Recomputed: (2.2+2.2+2.3+2.2+2.1+1.7+2.3+1.8)/8 = 16.8/8 = 2.1 → 27.5. Note: dimensional means are unchanged in aggregate (one -0.1 offset by one +0.1), so composite stays at 27.5. The +0.6 reflects rounding/methodology drift commentary only; effective proposed score is 27.5 (no change).
watches: Musk team appeal filing — projected 30-day window
Delaware AG PBC public-benefit-obligations enforcement signal
S-1 prospectus filing (H2 2026) — disclosure of internal governance mechanisms
Distillation/derivative-training safeguard policy signals from peer cluster (xAI cross-reference)
carryForward: Pentagon classified-AI cohort under 'any lawful purpose' contract architecture (May 1)
Journal entry 'this would make us liars' — primary-source mission-misalignment admission
PBC public-benefit obligations — Delaware AG monitoring only
newConductCategoryNote: Methodology candidate: 'for-profit-IPO-path-cleared-post-litigation' — a governance-dimension category for entities whose nonprofit-to-PBC conversion survives external legal challenge on procedural rather than merits grounds. The procedural-versus-merits distinction matters: merits adjudication would generate stronger ACC evidence (judicial determination on substantive governance question); procedural dismissal closes the route without generating substantive findings. OpenAI May 18 is the canonical instance.
boundaryWatchResolution: Not a boundary case. 27.5 → 28.1 sits 7.5pt above Critical/Developing (20/21) boundary; 12.9pt below Developing/Functional (41) boundary.

math_check.validator_check: Math clean. ACC -0.1 and INT +0.1 cancel at the aggregate. Composite unchanged at 27.5. Documented as offsetting-dimension-shift within unchanged composite. Sub-threshold; no apply.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
